summary.intent: Add default bundled skills to Moltis by copying vetted skills from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, with category UI, format fallback, and web UI visibility.
summary.outcome: 101 bundled skills (from Hermes + OpenClaw) embedded in the binary via include_dir!, with category metadata, web UI category filter, prompt format fallback, origin tracking, PR review fixes, and full web UI click-through for bundled skill details.

repo: Moltis uses include_dir! for embedded assets (same as web UI), feature-gated under 'bundled-skills' defaulting to on
SkillSource enum is used in exhaustive matches in discover.rs and watcher.rs — new variants require adding arms in both
The /api/skills web endpoint (crates/web/src/api.rs) creates its own FsSkillDiscoverer independently from the gateway, so bundled skills must be wired there separately
skill_detail RPC handler has per-source branches; bundled source needed its own branch reading from BundledSkillStore
Style.css is a generated Tailwind output — merge conflicts should be resolved by regenerating, not manually merging
Directory names for bundled skills must match the frontmatter 'name' field exactly; mismatches cause silent read failures in find_skill_dir lookups
Feature flags must be explicitly forwarded through each crate in the dependency chain (skills → tools → gateway → cli, and skills → web → cli)
mlops skills have 3-level nesting (mlops/training/axolotl/SKILL.md) requiring recursive directory traversal in both FS and embedded discovery

code.finding: BundledSkillStore uses include_dir! in release mode and direct filesystem reads in dev mode (same three-tier pattern as web assets); discovery must be recursive to handle nested categories
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled with name deduplication using a HashSet; must also deduplicate within the bundled set itself (not just cross-set)
Three-tier format fallback: full (name+desc+attrs) → compact (self-closing, no desc) → truncated (binary search for largest prefix); header changes to signal compact mode
skill_detail dispatches to source-specific functions; bundled source needs skill_detail_bundled reading from BundledSkillStore
api_skills_handler was creating FsSkillDiscoverer with only Personal+Project paths; must use CompositeSkillDiscoverer to expose bundled skills in web UI
read_bundled must call normalize_relative_skill_file_path() before passing to the store to prevent path traversal in dev-mode filesystem fallback

workflow: Python script injection of YAML frontmatter is more reliable than sed for multi-line blocks; sed truncates files when pattern spans lines
Greptile automated review caught real security issues (godmode bundled by default, path traversal, CDN integrity) — worth waiting for it before merging
When copying skills from another project, check for directory name vs frontmatter name mismatches — they cause silent failures that tests catch
Re-copy source files before running cleanup scripts to avoid operating on already-corrupted data
Tests should assert both count lower bounds and that every discovered skill is actually readable — catches name/path mismatches

friction: sed-based YAML frontmatter injection corrupted files by truncating content — required re-copy and switch to Python
Background bash tasks with jq parsing caused stuck polling loops; had to re-fetch results directly
Feature flag forwarding required touching 4+ Cargo.toml files (skills, tools, gateway, web, cli) — easy to miss one
Disk space issues on the worktree prevented full CLI build/test cycles; had to rely on per-crate checks
Arc import lint error in services.rs after replacing std::sync::Arc::new with Arc::new — existing import was already in scope
Tailwind CSS style.css merge conflicts on every main merge — entire file conflicts since it's fully generated
Node modules not installed in worktree required npm install before TypeScript type-checking or Vite builds
GitHub GraphQL API for resolving review threads required careful mutation construction to avoid shell word-splitting on thread IDs
open_items: Hermes-specific body text still remains in some skills (e.g., '~/.hermes/' paths, 'Hermes Integration' sections in github/* skills) — noted as cosmetic follow-up
Prompt format compact fallback would kick in around 300-400 skills — no char budget config exposed yet, hardcoded at 30KB
red-teaming/godmode removed entirely; could be re-added as opt-in non-default skill if useful
OpenClaw platform-specific extensions (Feishu, QQBot, ACP) and internal skills (clawflow, clawhub) not bundled — could be added if Moltis adds those integrations
Skills search in web UI added at end of session but not explicitly tested for correctness
Large sidecar reference files (unsloth/references/llms-full.md at 16.8k lines, powerpoint XSD schemas) bloat the binary — could be trimmed if binary size becomes a concern
No mechanism to update bundled skills independently of a full binary release — skills are frozen at compile time
